name: Alliant Energy Corporation

profile.description: Alliant Energy is a regulated electric and natural gas utility serving approximately one million electric and 425,000 natural gas customers across Iowa and Wisconsin through its Interstate Power and Light and Wisconsin Power and Light subsidiaries. The company is investing heavily in renewables, energy storage, and serving the surging data center load.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Alliant has executed a clear strategic pivot away from coal toward renewables, achieving 42% CO2 reduction vs 2005, retiring 1,100 MW of coal, and adding 1.2 GW of wind with another ~1,500 MW of solar plus 75 MW of storage in the pipeline [1][3]. AI Deployment (1/2): No customer-facing AI products, but the company is capitalizing on AI-driven demand by contracting 3 GW of data center load, a 50% increase in peak demand by 2030 [1]. Market Share (0/2): Regulated monopoly in its service territories in Iowa and Wisconsin, but this is a geographic franchise rather than competitive leadership. Pipeline (2/2): Capex plan of $3.13B (2026), $3.58B (2027), $3.63B (2028), $3.07B (2029) targets renewables, storage, gas, and T&D — specific multi-year visibility [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): Regulated utilities do not operate meaningful R&D; rate base investment is the equivalent lever.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Alliant's 'Clean Energy Blueprint' articulates a specific vision of decarbonizing while keeping rates affordable and serving communities — more than boilerplate and backed by the 42% CO2 reduction achievement [3]. Community Impact (1/2): Alliant Energy Foundation runs community grants and the utility participates in DE&I workforce measurement linked to short-term comp for over a decade, but dollar amounts are modest [3]. Employee Retention (0/2): Glassdoor culture score of 2.3/5 and overall 2.4/5 across 415 reviews is 34% below utilities-industry average, with upper management described as 'out of touch' [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): Ownership is modest; no major buying spikes reported, but CEO Lisa Barton has grown equity exposure since assuming CEO role in January 2024 [2].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Alliant is a classic capital-intensive regulated utility: leverage is high (D/E around 160-170%), ROE in the 10-11% range, and revenue growth is modest (~9.6% in 2025 on rate case outcomes and load growth) [1]. Forward P/E of ~18x on 2026 guidance of $3.36-$3.46 sits near the target, and the ~3.3% yield is supported by a consistently-growing dividend with 22+ years of growth [1]. Dividend growth CAGR of ~6% roughly matches the long-term EPS growth algorithm. The 10-year EPS CAGR of 6% is steady but unexciting, and payout ratio sits in the mid-60s — typical for utilities. Liquidity easily cleared (>2M daily volume).

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Regulated electric and gas delivery is structurally immune to AI substitution — you cannot replace wires, pipes, and generation with a language model. In fact AI is a demand tailwind: Alliant has contracted 3 GW of data center load, representing a ~50% peak-demand increase by 2030 that translates directly into rate base growth [1]. AI Opportunity (1/1): The data center contracts are hard-dollar revenue opportunities tied to AI compute expansion and will drive capex recovery and earnings for the next decade [1].

infinite_game.summary: Alliant has made a genuine, multi-decade commitment to the clean-energy transition in its service territories and has delivered consistent 6% EPS growth for 10 years. But the culture scores, regulated-monopoly comfort, and absence of bold strategic pivots keep it in the mixed category for Sinek.

just_cause.analysis: Alliant's Clean Energy Blueprint — to decarbonize while keeping energy affordable and reliable for Iowa and Wisconsin communities — is a genuine cause that has shaped capital allocation for more than a decade (42% CO2 reduction vs 2005, major coal retirements, 1.2 GW of wind online) [3]. It is lived out in strategic choices, even if the regulated structure means the 'courage' is partly rate-case driven.

trusting_teams.analysis: Glassdoor employee rating of 2.4/5 (culture 2.3/5) is 34% below the utilities industry average, and multiple reviews cite upper management as disconnected from the workforce [2]. This is a clear low-trust signal even for a stable regulated utility.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Alliant benchmarks against regional peers like WEC, Xcel, and MidAmerican on clean-energy transition and data-center attraction [1]. Management commentary is collaborative rather than combative, but the regulated monopoly structure limits real competitive pressure.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The coal-to-renewables transition is a genuine business-model shift for Alliant — retiring 1,300 MW of coal by 2024 while adding ~1,500 MW of solar and storage represents an incremental but meaningful reinvention of the generation fleet [3]. The data center pivot adds a new growth engine beyond traditional load growth.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Lisa Barton's team has committed to a $13B+ capex cycle through 2029 and pulled forward renewables despite federal policy uncertainty [1]. Tying DE&I and diversity metrics to executive compensation for more than a decade predates the current wave of ESG debate [3]. Not transformational, but above-average conviction.

investment_thesis: Alliant Energy is a bull case built on AI-driven load growth meeting a regulated utility chassis. Management reaffirmed 2026 EPS guidance of $3.36-$3.46 — roughly 6.6% growth at the midpoint — while adding contracted data center demand of 3 GW, a 50% increase in peak load demand by 2030 [1]. The 2026-2029 capex plan of $13.4B ($3.13B, $3.58B, $3.63B, $3.07B) translates that load into rate-base growth at authorized returns, and the 3.3% dividend yield backed by 22+ years of growth and a 6.1% 5-year dividend CAGR offers a steady total-return profile [1]. The Clean Energy Blueprint — 42% CO2 reduction vs 2005, 1.2 GW of wind, ~1,500 MW of solar plus 75 MW of storage in pipeline — adds ESG credibility and positions LNT well for corporate buyers of clean power [3].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on valuation, culture, and execution. Forward P/E of ~18x is close to historical highs for a utility growing EPS at 6% per year, leaving limited margin for rate-case disappointments or capex overruns. Debt-to-equity around 160-170% is typical for utilities but magnifies exposure to sustained higher real rates. Glassdoor employee rating of 2.4/5 (culture 2.3/5) — 34% below utilities-industry peers with upper management cited as disconnected from employees — is a concerning signal for an organization taking on a $13B+ capex cycle and onboarding major data center customers [2]. Data center demand could prove lumpy if hyperscaler capex plans are rationalized. Finally, the Inspire Insight misaligned label (-49) reflects disagreement over cultural/social policy and may deter values-aligned investors.
key_catalysts: Execution of 3 GW of contracted data center load through 2030, driving a 50% step-up in peak demand and rate-base growth [1]
2026-2029 capex of $13.4B flowing into rate base at authorized ROEs, supporting the 6% long-term EPS growth algorithm [1]
Continuation of coal retirements and renewables build-out (1,300 MW coal out by 2024; ~1,500 MW solar + 75 MW storage in) lowering fuel exposure [3]
key_risks: Regulatory risk in Iowa and Wisconsin rate cases — denied recovery or cost disallowances on the $13B+ capex plan would compress ROE [1]
Persistent Glassdoor ratings of 2.4/5 overall and 2.3/5 on culture, 34% below industry peers, suggest execution risk on a complex multi-year transition [2]
Data center demand concentration — hyperscaler capex cuts or siting shifts could lower the 3 GW contracted load pipeline [1]
sources: Alliant Energy Q3 2025 Earnings Release and 2026 Guidance, Businesswire, November 2025
Glassdoor — Alliant Energy Reviews (415 reviews, 2.4/5 overall, 2.3/5 culture), accessed April 2026
Alliant Energy 2023 Corporate Responsibility Report and Clean Energy Blueprint, alliantenergy.com
